skill.name: time-zone-planner
skill.description: Plan cross-time-zone meeting windows for distributed teams, providing region-by-region local time mappings and tradeoff analysis for scheduling decisions.
skill.summary: Cross-time-zone meeting planner for distributed teams with scheduling tradeoff analysis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 43 static analyzer findings are false positives. The 26 external_commands findings are markdown code block documentation examples, not actual shell execution. The 15 cryptographic algorithm findings are hash string metadata in audit result files, not real cryptography. The 2 hardcoded URL findings point to the skill's own GitHub repository. The only executable file (scripts/main.py) is a simple Python script with hardcoded test data and no network, subprocess, or filesystem operations. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub Source URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a hardcoded URL pointing to the skill's own GitHub source repository. This is a benign reference link, not a data exfiltration or C2 indicator. No action required.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Block Interpreted as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged all backtick code blocks and inline code in SKILL.md, POLISH_CHANGELOG.md, and references/audit-reference.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are documentation examples inside markdown code fences or inline formatting backticks. The only executable file (scripts/main.py) contains no subprocess, os.system, or exec calls. All 26 external_commands findings are false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hash String Misidentified as Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged SHA256 content hash values in time-zone-planner_audit_result_v2.json and POLISH_CHANGELOG.md as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. These are metadata identifiers and evaluation notes, not cryptographic implementations. No actual encryption or hashing code exists in the skill. All 15 blocker findings are false positives.

content.user_title: Plan Distributed Team Meetings Across Time Zones
content.value_statement: Distributed teams struggle to find meeting times that work across multiple time zones. This skill analyzes participant regions and constraints to recommend optimal meeting windows with local time mapping and tradeoff analysis.
actual_capabilities: Suggest optimal meeting windows across multiple time zones based on participant regions
Map meeting times to local time for each participant region automatically
Analyze scheduling tradeoffs between competing candidate windows
Document DST assumptions and limitations for each recommendation
Validate inputs and request missing information before proposing schedules
Accept IANA timezone names and region aliases with documented mappings
limitations: Does not check live calendar availability or book calendar events
Does not validate current DST status from live internet sources
Does not decide business priority between teams without user-defined rules
Uses representative timezone mappings for region aliases, not precise geolocation

use_cases.title: Quick Cross-Time-Zone Meeting Finder
use_cases.description: Enter participant regions and meeting duration to get recommended windows with local time mapping for each region.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads scheduling recurring meetings across global offices

use_cases.title: Scheduling Tradeoff Comparison
use_cases.description: Compare two or more candidate meeting windows and review tradeoffs including local hour impact for each option.
use_cases.target_user: Project managers evaluating multiple scheduling options for distributed sprint ceremonies

use_cases.title: Multi-Region Workshop Planning
use_cases.description: Plan workshops spanning three or more time zones with preferred hour constraints specified per region.
use_cases.target_user: Event coordinators organizing synchronous sessions for global teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Meeting Window
prompt_templates.prompt: Find a meeting window for regions US/Eastern, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo with a 60-minute duration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple one-off meeting across three time zones

prompt_templates.title: Meeting with Preferred Hours
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a 45-minute meeting for US/Pacific, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Singapore with preferred hours US/Pacific 9 to 17, Europe/Berlin 8 to 16, Asia/Singapore 9 to 18.
prompt_templates.scenario: Scheduling a recurring meeting with regional office hours respected

prompt_templates.title: Tradeoff Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare two candidate windows for US/Eastern, Australia/Sydney, India/IST. Option A is 9 AM Eastern. Option B is 1 PM Eastern. Analyze tradeoffs for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing between early morning and afternoon options for a cross-Pacific sync

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Window Constraint Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the best meeting windows for 5 regions: US/Pacific, US/Eastern, Europe/London, India/IST, Asia/Tokyo. Duration 90 minutes. Apply default business hours per region. Show the optimal window plus two alternatives with full tradeoff analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a global all-hands across five time zones with constraint analysis

output_examples.input: Find a meeting window for US/Eastern, Europe/London, and Asia/Tokyo teams with a 60-minute duration.
output_examples.output:
Optimal Window: 14:00-15:00 UTC
US/Eastern: 09:00-10:00 EST, within business hours
Europe/London: 14:00-15:00 GMT, midday
Asia/Tokyo: 23:00-00:00 JST, late evening
Tradeoffs: Asia/Tokyo falls outside standard business hours, but this is the only window where all three regions have partial overlap.
DST Note: Times assume standard time. Verify DST status for each region.

output_examples.input: The user asks: Can you book a calendar event for my team meeting at 3 PM tomorrow?
output_examples.output: This skill cannot book calendar events. The time-zone-planner skill is designed to recommend meeting windows across time zones for distributed teams. To proceed, please provide a list of participant regions and a meeting duration, and I will suggest optimal times with local time mappings.

output_examples.input: Compare two options for a US/Pacific and Europe/Berlin team meeting. Option A is 9 AM Pacific. Option B is 11 AM Pacific.
output_examples.output:
Option A: 9 AM Pacific is 18:00 Berlin. Berlin is at end of business day. Good for overlapping end of day.
Option B: 11 AM Pacific is 20:00 Berlin. Berlin is outside business hours. Not recommended.
Recommendation: Option A provides partial overlap within reasonable hours for both regions.

best_practices: Use IANA timezone names for precise mapping instead of region aliases like US or EU.
Specify preferred local hour ranges for each region to get more relevant recommendations.
Review and confirm tradeoffs and DST assumptions before sending calendar invites.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for live calendar availability checks or automated booking of events.
Do not rely on DST calculations without verifying current daylight saving status for each region.
Do not skip the input validation step by fabricating region data or meeting times.

faq.question: What information do I need to start?
faq.answer: You need a list of participant regions using IANA timezone names for best accuracy and the desired meeting duration in minutes.

faq.question: Can this skill book my calendar events?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides scheduling recommendations only. You must manually create calendar events using your preferred calendar tool.

faq.question: Does this skill check live DST status?
faq.answer: No. This skill documents DST assumptions but does not validate current DST status from live internet sources.

faq.question: How are region aliases like US or EU handled?
faq.answer: Short aliases are mapped to representative timezones: US to US/Eastern, EU to Europe/London, Asia to Asia/Shanghai. For accuracy, use specific IANA timezone names.

faq.question: What happens if the request is outside the skill scope?
faq.answer: The skill will refuse requests outside its scope, such as calendar booking or live availability checks, and explain which tool or action would be appropriate.

faq.question: Can I compare multiple meeting window options?
faq.answer: Yes. You can request tradeoff analysis comparing two or more candidate windows with local time mapping and constraint evaluation for each option.
